content.problemStatement:
Chef Ada constructed a simple undirected graph GGG with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) as follows:


Start with a complete graph with KKK vertices and K‚čÖ(K‚ąí1)/2K \cdot (K-1)/2K‚čÖ(K‚ąí1)/2 edges.
Then, add N‚ąíKN-KN‚ąíK vertices one by one. After adding each vetex, find some clique of K‚ąí1K-1K‚ąí1 vertices in the graph and connect the new vertex to all the vertices of that clique.
Consider NNN boolean variables X1,X2,‚Ä¶,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1‚Äč,X2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,XN‚Äč and their negations ¬¨X1,¬¨X2,‚Ä¶,¬¨XN\neg X_1, \neg X_2, \ldots, \neg X_N¬¨X1‚Äč,¬¨X2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,¬¨XN‚Äč, corresponding to vertices of the graph. You are given the initial values of all variables.

A CNF boolean expression is defined as a conjunction of clauses, where each clause is a disjunction of two or more elements of the set of variables and their negations.

Using the graph GGG, Ada wrote a CNF boolean expression BBB with MMM clauses, where each clause corresponds to a clique in the graph with some (maybe none) of the variables (corresponding to vertices) negated. More formally, for each iii from 111 to MMM:


Ada chose some clique in the graph, consisting of LiL_iLi‚Äč vertices Z1,Z2,‚Ä¶,ZLiZ_1, Z_2, \ldots, Z_{L_i}Z1‚Äč,Z2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,ZLi‚Äč‚Äč
then for each valid jjj, Ada chose a variable Pi,jP_{i,j}Pi,j‚Äč as either XZjX_{Z_j}XZj‚Äč‚Äč or ¬¨XZj\neg X_{Z_j}¬¨XZj‚Äč‚Äč
the iii-th clause in the expression BBB had LiL_iLi‚Äč terms: Pi,1‚ą®Pi,2‚ą®‚Ä¶‚ą®Pi,LiP_{i,1} \lor P_{i,2} \lor \ldots \lor P_{i,L_i}Pi,1‚Äč‚ą®Pi,2‚Äč‚ą®‚Ä¶‚ą®Pi,Li‚Äč‚Äč
Ada wants the expression to evaluate to "true". In order to achieve that, she may change the values of variables; for each valid iii, the cost of changing the value of XiX_iXi‚Äč (from "true" to "false" or vice versa) is CiC_iCi‚Äč. Find the minimum total cost needed to make the expression BBB evaluate to "true" or determine that it is impossible.
constraints: each character of SSS is either '0' or '1'
1‚Č§Li‚Č§N1 \le L_i \le N1‚Č§Li‚Äč‚Č§N for each valid iii
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (25 points, time limit 1 second):** K=3K = 3K=3
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
The second line contains a single binary string SSS with length NNN describing the initial values of the variables. For each valid iii, the iii-th character of SSS is '1' if XiX_iXi‚Äč is true or '0' if XiX_iXi‚Äč is false.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äč,C2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äč.
MMM lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains an integer LiL_iLi‚Äč followed by a space and LiL_iLi‚Äč space-separated integers representing the variables Pi,1,‚Ä¶,Pi,LiP_{i,1}, \ldots, P_{i,L_i}Pi,1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Pi,Li‚Äč‚Äč. For each valid iii, the variable XiX_iXi‚Äč is represented by the integer iii and its negation is represented by ‚ąíi-i‚ąíi.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the minimum cost needed to make the boolean expression evaluate to "true", or ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if it is impossible.

Explanation:
Example case 1: The following figure depicts the graph used to generate the expression.

The boolean expression is B=(X1‚ą®¬¨X2‚ą®X4)‚ąß(X2‚ą®X3‚ą®¬¨X5)‚ąß(X1‚ą®¬¨X3‚ą®X2)B = (X_1 \lor \neg X_2 \lor X_4) \land (X_2 \lor X_3 \lor \neg X_5) \land (X_1 \lor \neg X_3 \lor X_2)B=(X1‚Äč‚ą®¬¨X2‚Äč‚ą®X4‚Äč)‚ąß(X2‚Äč‚ą®X3‚Äč‚ą®¬¨X5‚Äč)‚ąß(X1‚Äč‚ą®¬¨X3‚Äč‚ą®X2‚Äč).
One optimal solution is to change the values of X1X_1X1‚Äč and X3X_3X3‚Äč.
